zh.lifeSummary: 當Countess Beatrice de Mandeville - Countess of Lincoln, Co Heiress於1209年出生在英國林可爾史爾林肯時，她的父親Alfred de Lincoln45歲，母親Maud Fitz Roy40歲。她和William de Gouvis至少有3個兒子。她去世於1265年10月25日在英國英格蘭多瑟特維姆斯聖 馬恩斯享年56歲。

nameMeanings.name: Lincoln,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from the city of Lincoln, so named from an original British name Lindo- ‘lake’ + Latin colonia ‘settlement, colony’. The place was an important administrative center during the Roman occupation of Britain and in the Middle Ages it was a center for the manufacture of cloth, including the famous ‘Lincoln green’.
African American: adoption of the surname in 1 above, in honor of Abraham Lincoln, the 16th president of the US, who abolished slavery.
History: Abraham Lincoln (1809–65), 16th president of the US, was the son of an illiterate laborer, descended from Samuel Lincoln, who had emigrated from England to MA in 1637.

variants.name: Linkins
meanings: English: variant of Linkin, which itself is a form of Lincoln , prevalent in Kent.

variants.name: Lindsay
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Lindsey in Lincolnshire, England. This is first found in the form Lindissi, apparently a derivative of the British name of Lincoln . To this was later added the Old English element ēg ‘island’, since the place was virtually cut off by the surrounding fenland. The surname was taken to Scotland at an early date and is the name of an important and powerful Scottish family.
Irish: adopted as an equivalent ofvarious Gaelic names (see   Lindsey  ).

nameMeanings.name: Beatrice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and French form of   Beatrix  , which was quite popular in England during the Middle Ages, and strongly revived in the 19th century. It is most famous as the name of Dante's beloved, and is borne by the elder (  b. 1988 ) of the Duke of York's daughters.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: ‘Bright’.

nameMeanings.name: Mandeville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Gabrielle, Camille, Emile, Fernand, Fernande, Gaston, Jean-Paul, Lucien, Michel.

French: habitational name from Mandeville, the name of two places in Normandy, derived from Latin magna villa ‘large estate’, or a variant of Manneville, a habitational name from Manneville, the name of several places in Normandy (see 2 below). Compare Montville and Monville .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Manneville (Seine-Maritime, Calvados), Manneville-sur-Risle (Eure), or Magneville (Manche).

variants.name: Mansfield
meanings: English: habitational name from a place so named in Nottinghamshire. The placename means ‘open land by the river Maun’, from the river name Maun (which may take its name from a hill named with Old English mamme ‘teat’) + Old English feld ‘open country’.
Irish (Waterford): when not an importation of 1, this is an altered form of the Norman name de Manville (see Mandeville 3).
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Mansfeld, a habitational name from a place so called in Saxony.

variants.name: Manderville
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Mandeville  .

variants.name: Manwell
meanings: Transferred use of the Scottish surname, in origin a local name from a minor place on the River Tweed, named as ‘the stream (Old English well(a)) of Mack’. The latter is a form of Magnus . Maxwell was the middle name of the newspaper tycoon William Maxwell Aitken , Lord Beaverbrook ( 1879–1964 ), who was born in Canada, and it has been used as a given name among his descendants. It is now also frequently taken as an expansion of Max .

variants.name: Manville
meanings: English (Sussex and Lancashire): variant of   Mandeville  .
Altered form of   Mainville   1, a surname of Swiss French origin (see   Miville  ).
French: variant of Menville, a habitational name from Menville in Haute-Garonne, and also of Mainville 2.

variants.name: Mundell
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin): variant of   Mandeville  .
Americanized form of German Mündel (or some other similar, like-sounding surname): nickname for someone with a prominent mouth, from Middle High German munt, mündelīn ‘(little) mouth’.

variants.name: Monville
meanings: Altered form of French   Mandeville  .
Walloon: habitational name from Monville in Namur province in Wallonia (Belgium), of the same etymology as in 3 below.
In some cases possibly also French: habitational name from Montville in Seine-Maritime or Monville in Calvados, both named with Old French mont ‘mountain, hill’ + ville ‘settlement’.

variants.name: Mandal
meanings: variant of Mendel , but assumed by folk etymology to be from German Männl, a diminutive of Mann ‘man’. It probably has no connection with the Yiddish vocabulary word mandel ‘almond’.

variants.name: Montville
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Vernice.
Altered form of French   Mandeville  .
French: variant of   Monville  . This surname is very rare in France, found mainly in Creuse.

variants.name: Manfull
meanings: English (Midlands): probably a variant of   Mandeville  . Compare   Manville  .

nameMeanings.name: Countess
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire, of Norman origin): from Middle English contas(e), Old French contesse ‘countess’, applied as a nickname for a proud, haughty woman or for a foppish man, or as an occupational name for a servant of a countess.

variants.name: Countiss
meanings: English: variant of   Countess  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Countess Beatrice de Mandeville - Countess of Lincoln, Co Heiress was born in 1209, in Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England, her father, Alfred de Lincoln, was 45 and her mother, Maud Fitz Roy, was 40. She had at least 3 sons with William de Gouvis. She died on 25 October 1265, in St Martin's, Weymouth, Dorset, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 56.

ko.lifeSummary: Countess Beatrice de Mandeville - Countess of Lincoln, Co Heiress(이)가 1209년 영국 링코실르 링코컨에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Alfred de Lincoln(은)는 45세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud Fitz Roy(은)는 40세였다. 그녀는 William de Gouvis(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1265년 10월 25일 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트 w이모우스 스트 마틴'스에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Countess Beatrice de Mandeville - Countess of Lincoln, Co Heiress родилась в 1209 г. в местности линколн, линколншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Alfred de Lincoln было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud Fitz Roy, 40. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына от William de Gouvis. Она умерла 25 октября 1265 г. в местности ст мартин'с, вимоут, дорсет, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 56 лет.

Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos con William de Gouvis. Murió el 25 de octubre de 1265, en St Martin's, Weymouth, Dorset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 56 años.
